blocks.heading: Learn A2UI + MCP
blocks.subtext: Google's real A2UI protocol, grounded in its own spec — and exactly where a2uicatalog sits relative to it. Seven modules, each its own self-contained page.

blocks.title: Grounded in the real spec, not this catalogue's own dialect
blocks.text: A2UI is Google's open protocol (a2ui.org, Apache 2.0) — a2uicatalog.ai is an independent, third-party implementation built on it, not the protocol itself. Every protocol claim in this brief traces to the real v1.0 specification; module 1 opens with the distinction, and module 7 closes with exactly how a2uicatalog relates to it.

modules.title: What A2UI actually is
modules.description: Real attribution, the trust-boundary problem it solves, version reality, and where a2uicatalog fits

modules.title: The message envelope
modules.description: All 9 real message types, a verbatim spec example, and progressive rendering

modules.title: Components & catalogs
modules.description: The adjacency-list model, the real 18-component Basic Catalog, and what catalogId actually is

modules.title: Data binding, paths & scope
modules.description: JSON Pointer paths, root vs collection scope, two-way binding, formatString

modules.title: Actions, functions & the LLM loop
modules.description: action/actionResponse, callFunction/functionResponse, execution boundaries, the generate-validate loop

modules.title: Transports & the MCP relationship
modules.description: Transport-agnostic by design, and the real A2UI <-> MCP Apps integration patterns

modules.title: a2uicatalog in the ecosystem
modules.description: A live-checked, honest look at where this catalogue sits relative to the real protocol

blocks.text: Built with the A2UI Catalog — 468 typed atoms, an independent implementation of Google's A2UI protocol. Browse the vocabulary at a2uicatalog.ai.
